message:
perf(fcp-webhook): switch DeadLetterQueue from Vec to VecDeque for O(1) FIFO eviction

The webhook DeadLetterQueue used a `Vec<WebhookEvent>` and called
`events.remove(0)` whenever the queue exceeded `max_size`, which is
O(n) on each insertion past the cap because every remaining element
gets shifted left. Under sustained dead-letter pressure (many failing
webhooks during a provider outage) this turned eviction into a hot
path that scales linearly with the cap.

Fix: replace the backing store with `VecDeque<WebhookEvent>`. Eviction
now uses `pop_front()` and insertion uses `push_back()`, both O(1).
Public method behavior is unchanged:

  - `push(event)`: still evicts the oldest event if full, then inserts
    the new one at the back (FIFO order preserved).
  - `all()`: still returns `Vec<WebhookEvent>` — the impl now collects
    `events.read().iter().cloned()` instead of `events.read().clone()`
    so the public type stays identical.
  - `remove(event_id)`: VecDeque::remove returns `Option<T>`, so the
    `Some(events.remove(pos))` wrapper is no longer needed; the call
    flows through unchanged.

Test added in the same file:
  test_dead_letter_queue_evicts_oldest_when_full
  - Builds a DLQ with `max_size = 2`, pushes three events ("oldest",
    "middle", "newest"), and asserts the queue holds {"middle",
    "newest"} after the cap is exceeded — i.e. the oldest entry was
    evicted and the FIFO order is preserved.

No callers needed to change; `Vec` and `VecDeque` both expose the
iter / len / contains / remove-by-position surface this code uses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
